task_completion.description: Verify that a connection to the VistA database is established.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The YDBGui interface or a terminal shows an active, successful connection to the VistA/YottaDB environment.

task_completion.description: Verify that the YDBGui application is open and accessible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The YDBGui graphical user interface window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify navigation to the Person Class global or table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The YDBGui interface displays the global ^USC(8932.1) or the PERSON_CLASS table as selected or open in the viewer.

task_completion.description: Verify that the classification name 'Emergency Medicine' is located.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Emergency Medicine' is visible within the data records of the ^USC(8932.1) global or PERSON_CLASS table in YDBGui.

task_completion.description: Verify that the associated X12 Taxonomy Code is displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An X12 Taxonomy Code starting with '207P' (e.g., 207P00000X) is visible in the same record or row as 'Emergency Medicine' within the YDBGui data viewer.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the YDBGui interface to locate the information as requested, rather than relying solely on terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: The target term and code are displayed inside the YDBGui graphical application window, not just printed in a terminal emulator.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent interacted with the actual database records and did not just type the expected answers into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The results appear in the structured data grid, tree, or global viewer of YDBGui, rather than in a notepad, text editor, or standalone script file.

integrity.description: Verify that the taxonomy code is genuinely retrieved from the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: The taxonomy code '207P...' is shown as part of a database record in YDBGui, not as a standalone printed output or manually typed text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target global for the Person Class file is ^USC(8932.1). The target classification term is 'Emergency Medicine'. The correct X12 Taxonomy Code for Emergency Medicine has the prefix '207P' (specifically 207P00000X).
